about.help: HertzBeat's unified platform with AI intelligence, powerful customization, multi-type support, high performance, and easy expansion, aims to help users quickly and conveniently achieve observability needs.
about.not-show-next-login: Do not show this popup on next login
about.point.1: Collect metrics from applications, databases, operating systems, middleware, and network devices without sending data outside your deployment.
about.point.2: Receive OTLP logs with resource, entity, trace, and alert context so operators can continue troubleshooting from the log line.
about.point.3: Handle threshold and external alerts in one place with acknowledge, silence, inhibit, recover, and close actions.
about.point.4: Notify the channels your operations team uses and keep the handling record in HertzBeat.
about.point.5: Use monitoring templates to define collection protocols, metrics, and thresholds for new resource types.
about.point.6: Scale collector clusters across network zones for private deployment, isolated collection, and status pages.
about.title: Apache HertzBeat™ is an open-source private-deployable enterprise operations observability platform. Unify collectors, monitoring templates, metrics, logs, traces, and alert handling in one operations workspace.

alert.help.center: Use this page to review alerts after grouping, convergence, inhibition, and silence rules, including internal threshold alerts and third-party alerts.
alert.help.group:
Group convergence merges alerts by the selected labels and removes duplicates in the same time window. Click "Add grouping policy" to configure it.
Threshold alerts and third-party alerts both pass through this step to avoid alert storms.
alert.help.inhibit: Alert inhibit defines relationships between alerts. When one alert appears, it can suppress related alerts. For example, a server-down alert can suppress other alerts on that server.
alert.help.integration: Use this page to receive and manage alerts from third-party platforms, then continue with grouping, convergence, inhibition, silence, and notification delivery.
alert.help.notice:
Use notifications to configure receivers and delivery methods for alerts, including email, Discord, and webhook. View the setup steps.
"Notice Template" defines the message format for each delivery method. You can use the built-in template or create your own.
Note⚠️: After configuring the "Receiver", you still need a "Notice Policy" to decide who receives each message. View common issues.
alert.help.setting:
Threshold rules define when metrics should trigger alerts. Click "New Threshold" to configure a rule, and HertzBeat will trigger alerts from metrics.
Note⚠️: Triggered alerts appear in [Alert Center], and notification methods and receivers can be configured in [Notification].
alert.help.silence:
Use silence rules to pause notifications during maintenance windows or off-hours.
Click "New Silence Strategy" to configure the time range.

SQL Query Language

Examples:
• SELECT COUNT(*) FROM hertzbeat_logs WHERE severity_text = 'ERROR'
• SELECT service_name, COUNT(*) FROM hertzbeat_logs GROUP BY service_name
• SELECT * FROM hertzbeat_logs WHERE timestamp >= now() - INTERVAL '5 minutes'
